mnemonics: AlexNet (2012) Leaped ahead, VGG (2014) Went Very Deep, GoogLeNet (2014) was Inception-all, ResNet (2015) added a Residual path.
keyPoints: The key innovation was moving from simple, shallow networks (LeNet) to deeper architectures with techniques to solve the vanishing gradient problem.
Architectures evolved to use smaller filters (e.g., 3x3 in VGG) and more complex, efficient modules (e.g., Inception, Residual blocks).
The trend was driven by the availability of larger datasets (e.g., ImageNet) and more powerful computational resources like GPUs.
commonMistakes: Assuming newer architectures always completely replace older ones; they often build upon and integrate previous ideas.
Confusing the purpose of a Residual connection (for easing gradient flow) with a simple performance booster or skip connection.
